rationale: All 3 lenses ran; 5/6 acceptance criteria pass. Two major patch findings: a typo referencing a non-existent INSTALL_MODE variable (CR-001) and an over-certain macOS log-path claim that is unverifiable from this repo and mismatches the plan doc's file path (CR-002). Both are small factual fixes. Acceptance criterion 6 (verbatim preservation) is partial because the Defaults prose was edited to satisfy criterion 5 (name the modes) — resolved by design; substantive wizard/artifacts content is byte-identical.

business_review.criterion: 1. macOS .dmg in intro + distribution list + new wizard section
5. Name Windows portable vs macOS auto modes
6. Keep Windows wizard / Release Artifacts / Defaults verbatim
business_review.notes: Wizard + Release Artifacts byte-identical; Defaults prose edited to satisfy criterion 5 — resolved by design

standards_review.notes: docs(cli): ... matches Conventional Commits; scope 'cli' is in allowed enum (install was not)

findings.location: Script Options > macOS/Linux/WSL table, CODEMIE_NPM_PREFIX row
findings.problem: Row says 'npm prefix used when `INSTALL_MODE` resolves to `user-prefix`' but no INSTALL_MODE variable exists — the documented env var is CODEMIE_INSTALL_MODE.
findings.impact: A user may set a non-existent INSTALL_MODE and the user-prefix mode silently fails to trigger.

findings.problem: States as established fact that output goes to ~/Library/Logs/CodeMie/ (a directory), but the wizard source lives in a separate repo (codemie-claude-installer-mac) so this is unverifiable from here; the plan doc (docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-03-codemie-claude-installer-mac.md:597) specifies the FILE ~/Library/Logs/CodeMie/wizard.log.
findings.impact: Over-certain claim about a separate-repo artifact; directory-vs-file mismatch could mislead debugging.
findings.recommendation: Soften to attribution framing and reference the file path from the plan doc, e.g. 'Wizard output is written to ~/Library/Logs/CodeMie/wizard.log (per the macOS installer plan; the wizard source lives in a separate repository).'
